The recall involves approximately 212,000 lbs of liquid eggs and was issued on March 28, 2025. The deadline for closure is July 1, 2025, which gives a window of 95 days (about 3 months). This is categorized as a Class III recall (low health risk), which generally requires fewer effectiveness checks by the FSIS and can therefore be closed faster than Class I or II recalls. However, 212,000 lbs is a significant volume, meaning product retrieval and documentation by the firm will still take substantial time, and FSIS bureaucratic processes typically take 3-6 months to finalize closures. Given the 95-day window, there is a moderate chance the administrative process finishes in time, aided by the low-risk classification, but it's borderline due to typical bureaucratic delays. Therefore, I estimate a 55% likelihood.

The question specifies that the protest must have a start date *after the launch of this question*. The background explicitly links to the "2025 Turkish protests" Wikipedia article, meaning the question was launched *after* the massive March 19, 2025 protests following Ekrem Imamoglu's arrest. Therefore, those March protests (with hundreds of thousands of people) do NOT qualify, as their start date was before the question launched. A completely new protest wave exceeding 10,000 people would need to begin between approximately early April and late June. While the political climate in Turkey remains very tense, triggering a completely separate new >10k protest wave in a two-and-a-half month window is unlikely, leading to a 25% estimate. May Day (May 1) protests happened recently, but unless they escalate into a distinct massive wave, the tracker typically requires a specific novel trigger to log a new episode, and routine May Day clashes might not meet the strict 10k+ threshold or qualify as a new episodic tracker entry depending on how they unfolded.
